articles.title: Israeli forces kill three-year-old Palestinian boy on family farm in Gaza
articles.summary: Rayan Abu al-Ajeen was being carried in Gaza when both were shot by Israeli forces on Sunday, family says.
articles.titleZh: 以色列军队在加沙家庭农场杀死三岁巴勒斯坦男孩
articles.summaryZh: 拉扬·阿布·阿吉恩的家人称，周日，拉扬·阿布·阿吉恩在加沙被以色列军队开枪射杀。

contentParagraphs.en: Rayan Abu al-Ajeen was being carried in Gaza when both were shot by Israeli forces on Sunday, family says.Deir el-Balah, Gaza Strip – Israeli forces shot and killed a three-year-old boy as his father carried him in central Gaza, the family has said.Rayan Abu al-Ajeen was shot in the Wadi al-Salqa area of Deir el-Balah governorate on Sunday afternoon, and his body was taken to the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital on Monday.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Iran, US agree tentative deal to ‘end war’: Your questions answeredlist 2 of 3G7 meeting in France: What’s on agenda, who is attending?list 3 of 3Can you spend $1 trillion? We hand you Musk’s fortune to find outend of listHis father, Bahaa, was shot in the leg and is being treated in hospital.The family says the father and son had been travelling in areas outside of the so-called ‘Yellow Line’ – not under the direct control of the Israeli military – and were heading towards the family’s greenhouses when the shooting happened.Jaber Abu al-Ajeen, Rayan’s grandfather, says he was at home next to the family’s farm when he heard gunfire, and discovered that his son Bahaa and another relative he was walking with had been targeted. He then found out that Rayan had been killed.“My grandson, Rayan, was killed by a gunshot to the head; the bullet entered his head and exited through his eye,” Jaber told Al Jazeera. “His mother is devastated by what happened.”Al-Ajeen explained that his son had been taken into an area controlled by the Israeli army, and then left bleeding for seven hours until he was transferred to hospital for treatment.“We are still deeply worried about Bahaa’s condition, as he is not yet stable after bleeding for hours, and the condition of his leg is very serious.”The Israeli military has yet to comment on the shooting.Israeli forces have continued attacks in Gaza since the October ceasefire, killing almost 1,000 Palestinians. In total, Israel has killed more than 73,000 Palestinians since the beginning of its genocidal war on Gaza in October 2023.Many of the killings have been near the ‘Yellow Line’, which Israel has continued to expand despite the stipulations of the ceasefire agreement.Jaber al-Ajeen said the family had long been wary because of their proximity to the ‘Yellow Line’.“We have lived in this area for a long time,” he said. “We are mere civilians working in agriculture, and all our lands are located outside of the ‘Yellow Line’.
contentParagraphs.zh: 家人称，周日，拉扬·阿布·阿吉恩 (Rayan Abu al-Ajeen) 在加沙地带被以色列军队开枪射击。加沙地带代尔巴拉赫 - 家人称，以色列军队在加沙地带中部开枪打死了一名三岁男孩，当时他的父亲抱着他。拉扬·阿布·阿吉恩 (Rayan Abu al-Ajeen) 周日下午在代尔巴拉省的 Wadi al-Salqa 地区被枪杀，他的尸体被送往阿克萨烈士医院周一。推荐故事清单 3 项清单 1（共 3 项）伊朗、美国同意达成“结束战争”的临时协议：您的问题已回答清单 2 法国 3G7 会议的清单 2：议程内容是什么，谁出席？清单 3（共 3 项）你能花 1 万亿美元吗？我们将马斯克的财富交给您，让您找到名单之外的内容。他的父亲巴哈腿部中弹，正在医院接受治疗。家人称，父子俩一直在所谓的“黄线”之外的地区旅行——不受以色列军方的直接控制——枪击事件发生时，他们正在前往自家的温室。拉扬的祖父贾比尔·阿布·阿金说，当他听到枪声时，他正在自家农场旁边的家中，发现他的儿子巴哈和与他同行的另一位亲戚成为了攻击目标。然后他发现拉扬已被杀。“我的孙子拉扬因头部中弹而死亡；子弹进入他的头部并从他的眼睛射出，”贾比尔告诉半岛电视台。 “他的母亲对所发生的事情感到非常震惊。”阿金解释说，他的儿子被带到以色列军队控制的地区，然后流血了七个小时，直到被转移到医院接受治疗。“我们仍然对巴哈的病情深感担忧，因为他在流血几个小时后还不稳定，而且他的腿伤势非常严重。” 1,000 名巴勒斯坦人。自2023年10月对加沙发动种族灭绝战争以来，以色列总共杀害了超过73,000名巴勒斯坦人。其中许多杀戮发生在“黄线”附近，尽管有停火协议的规定，以色列仍在不断扩大黄线范围。贾比尔·阿金表示，一家人长期以来一直保持警惕，因为他们靠近“黄线”。“我们在这个地区生活了很长时间，”他说。 “我们只是务农的平民，我们所有的土地都位于‘黄线’之外。

articles.title: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet
articles.summary: The BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman on the big changes coming down the line for young people online.
articles.titleZh: 社交媒体禁令——大胆而直率，但没有灵丹妙药
articles.summaryZh: BBC 技术编辑佐伊·克莱曼 (Zoe Kleinman) 讲述年轻人上网即将发生的重大变化。

contentParagraphs.en: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet1 hour agoZoe KleinmanTechnology editorGetty ImagesWhen I arrived at 10 Downing Street this morning to hear the prime minister announce the UK's social media ban for under-16s, I had to hand in my phone for security reasons.The temporary anxiety it gave me was perhaps a small insight into how many of the nation's 13 to 15-year-olds were feeling, as they too waited to hear the outcome of months of discussion and speculation about their online lives.Sir Keir Starmer's news was bold and blunt: yes there will be a ban, yes it will follow Australia's model and yes, there will be additional curbs which will impact older children, aged 16 and 17, as well. A night-time curfew is expected to be part of this. The UK's plan had been dubbed "Australia Plus" and now we know why.But Australia's ban has faced well-documented problems, not least because the majority of children who had social media accounts before the ban was enforced in December, still have them. The response from the UK government is the same answer I hear every time there's an online safety intervention.There is talk that the ban will not be a "silver bullet" but will still make some difference.Sir Keir made several comparisons to laws about drinking alcohol: we know some teens under the age of 18 still drink, but we also know there are plenty of times when the law prevents it.But we also know 16-year-olds can legally get married, have sex, join the armed forces, and in some parts of the UK, vote in some elections. Can they not also be trusted on Instagram at night?Given how many horror stories I have heard during the course of my career about children coming to harm on gaming platforms and forums, it was surprising to learn that they will be exempt. However they will have to disable livestreaming, as that is included in the ban.The big question: how will it work?The big question of course is how this is actually going to work. I did ask the PM but he didn't directly tell me. Australia's failing is believed to be in weak age verification methods adopted by the tech companies.I've heard from a couple of fuming industry contacts today that they were under the impression that it would be up to Apple and Google to gatekeep devices (as most people own either an Apple or Android phone).This would mean when someone aged under 16 sets up a device in the UK, they can't download banned apps from the app stores.The people I spoke to are blindsided that this no longer appears to be the case and it will be down to individual platforms. And the deadline for them to figure out a better alternative is short: less than a year to meet the proposed implementation next spring.Other experts argue that toxic online behaviour is a societal rather than a technology problem, and the blunt instrument of blocking the tech altogether on its own is not the solution. "Right diagnosis, wrong cure" was the headline of one email in my inbox earlier today.A huge change for the online generationFacebook launched in the UK 21 years ago, and the other social networks soon followed suit.This means there's a generation of young people who have grown up in this world - warts and all. One reporter at the briefing with me made me feel very old by confiding that YouTube - included in the ban - had been an essential resource for them at school. I'm still haunted by a message I received from a teenager a short while ago who said without social media they would be dead, because the community they had discovered online had given them reasons to live. There are of course also many tragic examples of the exact opposite of this.Campaigners - and government ministers - say the tech companies have had years to do more to protect children, and have failed to do so. The companies argue they have introduced numerous parental controls and blocked access to features they know to be harmful. But Sir Nick Clegg, former senior exec at Meta, once said there were so many parental controls that parents were overwhelmed by them and didn't use them. I think many parents can relate to that.My sources say the tech companies won't fight the ban as long as they feel it is fair to all of them - but I'm not convinced all of them do feel that today. There's also concern that banning the big mainstream apps will push children into darker, less regulated corners of the internet with fewer or even zero protections in place.There's a chance the whole thing could end up in judicial review because of the speed at which it has been rolled out, just a couple of weeks after the closure of an enormous public consultation - which would probably delay it. Political questions for StarmerThere are definite "prime minister's legacy" vibes about the news, given the broader political picture at the moment.And finally, there might also be a Donald Trump-shaped obstacle in the road. Sir Keir hasn't spoken to him about this yet, but he will see him later on today at the G7 summit where he is now heading. Trump is protective of his US-based tech companies, and has railed in the past against attempts by other countries to regulate them. The US government responded to the public consultation insisting that a ban was not the solution.The same US companies Starmer now seeks to reign in are also investing millions of dollars in their UK operations and infrastructure. Tech and AI are key priorities in the government's plans for economic growth. As one former senior government adviser once put it to me, "there is no Plan B".The PM has to walk a very fine line between clamping down on them and selling the UK as a good place for them to be. We will see in the coming months whether any of them choose to vote with their feet.
contentParagraphs.zh: 社交媒体禁令 - 大胆而直率，但没有灵丹妙药一小时前佐伊·克莱曼（Zoe Kleinman）技术编辑盖蒂图片社当我今天早上到达唐宁街 10 号，听到首相宣布英国针对 16 岁以下青少年的社交媒体禁令时，出于安全原因，我不得不交出手机。它给我带来的暂时焦虑也许是对全国许多 13 至 15 岁青少年的感受的一个小小的了解，因为他们也在等待几个月的结果凯尔·斯塔默爵士的消息大胆而直白：是的，将会有一项禁令，是的，它将效仿澳大利亚的模式，是的，将会有额外的限制，这也会影响 16 岁和 17 岁的年龄较大的儿童。夜间宵禁预计将成为其中的一部分。英国的计划被称为“澳大利亚+”，现在我们知道原因了。但澳大利亚的禁令面临着有据可查的问题，尤其是因为在去年 12 月禁令实施之前拥有社交媒体账户的大多数儿童仍然拥有这些账户。英国政府的回应与我每次进行网络安全干预时听到的答案都是一样的。有传言称该禁令不会是“灵丹妙药”，但仍会产生一些影响。基尔爵士对有关饮酒的法律进行了多次比较：我们知道一些 18 岁以下的青少年仍然喝酒，但我们也知道法律很多时候会禁止饮酒。但我们也知道 16 岁的青少年可以合法结婚、发生性行为、加入武装部队，并且在某些情况下英国部分地区在某些选举中投票。难道他们晚上在 Instagram 上就不能被信任吗？考虑到我在职业生涯中听过多少关于儿童在游戏平台和论坛上受到伤害的恐怖故事，当得知他们将被豁免时，我感到很惊讶。然而，他们将不得不禁用直播，因为这已包含在禁令中。最大的问题：它将如何运作？当然，最大的问题是这实际上将如何运作。我确实问过总理，但他没有直接告诉我。据信，澳大利亚的失败在于科技公司采用的年龄验证方法不力。今天，我从一些愤怒的行业联系人那里听说，他们的印象是，苹果和谷歌将负责把关设备（因为大多数人拥有苹果或安卓手机）。这意味着，当 16 岁以下的人在英国设置设备时，他们无法从应用程序商店下载被禁止的应用程序。与我交谈的人措手不及，情况似乎不再是这样，而且将来也会如此。细化到各个平台。他们找出更好的替代方案的最后期限很短：不到一年的时间就能在明年春天实现拟议的实施。其他专家认为，有毒的在线行为是一个社会问题，而不是一个技术问题，完全阻止技术本身并不是解决方案。今天早些时候，我收件箱中的一封电子邮件的标题是“正确的诊断，错误的治疗”。对于在线一代来说，这是一个巨大的变化Facebook 21 年前在英国推出，其他社交网络很快也纷纷效仿。这意味着这个世界上已经成长了一代年轻人——尽管有缺点。与我一起参加吹风会的一位记者透露，YouTube（包括在禁令中）一直是他们在学校的重要资源，这让我感觉自己很老了。我仍然对不久前从一个青少年那里收到的一条消息耿耿于怀，他说如果没有社交媒体他们就会死，因为他们在网上发现的社区给了他们活下去的理由。当然，还有许多与此完全相反的悲惨例子。活动人士和政府部长表示，科技公司多年来一直在保护儿童方面采取更多措施，但未能做到这一点。这些公司辩称，他们引入了多种家长控制措施，并阻止访问他们知道有害的功能。但 Meta 前高级主管尼克·克莱格爵士曾表示，家长控制措施太多，以至于家长们不知所措，没有使用它们。我认为许多家长都可以理解这一点。我的消息来源说，只要科技公司认为这对所有人都公平，他们就不会反对这项禁令 - 但我不相信今天所有人都这么认为。还有人担心，禁止大型主流应用程序会将儿童推入互联网上更黑暗、监管更少、保护措施更少甚至为零的角落。由于其推出速度太快，整个事情有可能最终进入司法审查，而距离一次大规模的公众咨询结束仅几周——这可能会推迟它。斯塔默的政治问题考虑到目前更广泛的政治图景，这一消息显然带有“总理遗产”的氛围。最后，道路上可能还存在唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)型的障碍。凯尔爵士尚未与他谈及此事，但他将于今天晚些时候在他即将前往的七国集团峰会上见到他。特朗普保护他的美国科技公司，并在过去批评其他国家试图监管它们。美国政府对公众咨询作出回应，坚称禁令不是解决方案。斯塔默现在寻求控制的美国公司也在英国的业务和基础设施上投资了数百万美元。科技和人工智能是政府经济增长计划的重点优先事项。正如一位前高级政府顾问曾经对我说的那样，“没有 B 计划”。首相必须在压制他们和让英国成为他们的好地方之间走一条非常微妙的界限。我们将在接下来的几个月里看看他们中是否有人选择用脚投票。

articles.title: The US and Iran have agreed a deal. How soon could the economy go back to normal?
articles.summary: Experts warn the impact of the war will continue to affect the global economy for months to come.
articles.titleZh: 美国和伊朗已达成协议。经济多久才能恢复正常？
articles.summaryZh: 专家警告称，战争的影响将在未来几个月继续影响全球经济。

contentParagraphs.en: The US and Iran have agreed a deal. How soon could the economy go back to normal?1 hour agoFaarea Masud,Rachel Clun,Business reportersandJonathan JosephsReutersMore than three months after the US and Israel first began their war with Iran, the White House and the Iranian regime have agreed a framework deal to bring about a more long-term end to hostilities.The Middle East crisis sent global oil prices soaring as the conflict effectively closed one of the world's key water transport routes for oil, liquid natural gas and other essential commodities, limiting global supplies.But experts warn a return to normal shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will take time, and the impact of the war will continue to affect the global economy for potentially months to come.How quickly will the Strait of Hormuz reopen?"Let the oil flow!" US President Donald Trump said in a social media post heralding the agreement, which he said would include the reopening of the strait to commercial shipping.BBC Verify has been checking ship-tracking data which appears to show that traffic levels remain low in the Strait of Hormuz, despite the announcement.According to ship tracking website MarineTraffic, only two vessels with active location trackers have exited the waterway since Sunday - a bulk carrier and a tanker.The strait has been closed to most shipping traffic since 28 February, with only limited numbers of vessels friendly to Iran able to pass through.Hundreds of vessels have been stuck in the gulf, with the risk of sea mines or drone strikes driving up the danger to crews and preventing safe passage.Neil Shearing, group chief economist for Capital Economics, said it remained to be seen whether the latest deal "represents a fragile truce or a durable settlement".He added that it was likely it will "take some time for oil flows through the Strait to return to pre-war levels"."Even if ships now have safe passage, tankers are in the wrong place, oil production/refining facilities need to get up to full capacity, and questions over the cost and availability of insurance for ships traversing the Strait will remain," he said.Even before the agreement, during the ongoing ceasefire, shipping companies were largely reluctant to try to move their vessels out of the strait – and getting those vessels out will be their first focus.Denmark's Maersk is the world's second biggest shipping line and has five ships that have been stuck in the Gulf because of this conflict. The firm said it was too early to assess how the agreement "will impact logistics", and that for now, there is no change to its operations in the region.German shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd has four ships stuck in the strait and hopes to get them out over the weekend, once the deal is signed and any remaining mines are cleared.What does that mean for oil prices from here?Normally, about a fifth of the world's oil and LNG supplies flow through the strait, and the effective halt to traffic has increased oil prices. That in turn has had a knock-on effect on petrol, diesel and jet fuel costs.During the conflict, the price of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, peaked at around $120 a barrel, whereas before hostilities broke out it was just below $70.Following news of the framework deal, Brent fell to $83.55 a barrel.President Trump said that the Strait of Hormuz would open once the "deal" is signed on Friday. Senior energy strategist at Rabobank Florence Schmit said there was a "strong possibility that we'll see a lot of volatility" in the lead up to the deal being signed."Some things are not confirmed on both sides – important things: we don't know if the deal will be signed," she told the BBC."What we've seen so far is a deal for 60 days for the opening of the Strait – but what happens after that? What if the Iranians want to re-insert a toll system?"A full-scale peace agreement could still be a long way off." Despite that, Schmit said normality in the system, including prices, "could return by the end of the year" if a full ceasefire is agreed upon. Normality would include the return of pre-war levels of 26 daily crude oil tankers going through the strait.With the current positive news headlines and "sentiment-driven" sell-off, she said there is a chance the price could drop below $80 a barrel, but then it could average by end of year in the mid $80's again as "the geopolitics is stripped out" and the market assesses the reality of the situation.What could the impact be on food prices?Global food prices could also be helped if supplies of fertiliser get closer to normal levels again. Fertiliser, a by-product of oil, has soared in costs, putting pressure on farmers.Maurizio Carulli, global energy analyst at Quilter Cheviot, said the ceasefire "should help ease the immediate pressure on fertiliser markets" - but it won't be instant.He added that roughly a third of traded fertiliser and major volumes of natural gas, used for nitrogen-based fertiliser, flow through the Strait of Hormuz and that "lingering damage to energy infrastructure" will take time to repair."What's more, the crop season has already begun in several parts of the world, so the resumption of deliveries of nitrogen and phosphate fertilisers will be too late for agricultural crops, which will negatively affect global produce."Jet fuel - another by-product of oil - traded in the Northwest Europe (NWE) has already seen a small fall in price.NWE jet fuel is down to $1,033 per tonne, compared with $831 per tonne before the conflict, and around $1,840 at its peak.What other effects could we see from the US-Iran deal?The Iran war has affected economies around the world, as the jump in energy costs has pushed up fuel prices leading to a pick-up in inflation. This has put pressure on central banks to raise interest rates to keep inflation under control.In the UK, before the war began, the Bank of England had been widely expected to cut interest rates this year. But these predictions were quickly changed

Fertiliser, a by-product of oil, has soared in costs, putting pressure on farmers.Maurizio Carulli, global energy analyst at Quilter Cheviot, said the ceasefire "should help ease the immediate pressure on fertiliser markets" - but it won't be instant.He added that roughly a third of traded fertiliser and major volumes of natural gas, used for nitrogen-based fertiliser, flow through the Strait of Hormuz and that "lingering damage to energy infrastructure" will take time to repair."What's more, the crop season has already begun in several parts of the world, so the resumption of deliveries of nitrogen and phosphate fertilisers will be too late for agricultural crops, which will negatively affect global produce."Jet fuel - another by-product of oil - traded in the Northwest Europe (NWE) has already seen a small fall in price.NWE jet fuel is down to $1,033 per tonne, compared with $831 per tonne before the conflict, and around $1,840 at its peak.What other effects could we see from the US-Iran deal?The Iran war has affected economies around the world, as the jump in energy costs has pushed up fuel prices leading to a pick-up in inflation. This has put pressure on central banks to raise interest rates to keep inflation under control.In the UK, before the war began, the Bank of England had been widely expected to cut interest rates this year. But these predictions were quickly changed

articles.title: In Oregon, a newly discovered species of spider got its own name and naming ceremony
articles.summary: A biologist recently discovered a new species of cave spider in Oregon's Columbia River Gorge. It's getting a name from the Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakama Nation.
articles.titleZh: 在俄勒冈州，一种新发现的蜘蛛有了自己的名字和命名仪式
articles.summaryZh: 一位生物学家最近在俄勒冈州哥伦比亚河峡谷发现了一种新的洞穴蜘蛛。它的名字来自亚卡马民族联盟部落和乐队。

contentParagraphs.en: A biologist recently discovered a new species of cave spider in Oregon's Columbia River Gorge. It's getting a name from the Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakama Nation.
contentParagraphs.zh: A biologist recently discovered a new species of cave spider in Oregon's Columbia River Gorge. It's getting a name from the Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakama Nation.

contentParagraphs.en: DAVID FOLKENFLIK, HOST: There are about 54,000 species of spiders in the world, so it doesn't seem like finding just one more would be that big of a deal. But in Oregon, one new species received a ceremonial welcome. Deena Prichep reports.DEENA PRICHEP: A few years ago, Greta Binford was taking a hike in the Columbia River Gorge, and she saw a spider.GRETA BINFORD: And it just looked different from anything that I normally see when I roll over rocks and logs in this area.PRICHEP: Binford is an evolutionary biologist at Lewis & Clark College who studies spiders, hence rolling over rocks and logs. But this was not the spider she expected to see.BINFORD: And the first thing I thought was, wow. And it looks like trogloraptor.PRICHEP: Trogloraptor is a family of spiders that only contains one species, and that species lives about 350 miles away. Over the next few years, Binford found more of these spiders and discovered this was trogloraptor, but a new species. And when you discover a new species, you get to name it. Usually, it's a name that reflects the organism's characteristics or some source of inspiration. Naming is an honor, and Binford's lab decided to share it.BINFORD: We had an idea really spearheaded by my student, Madeline Jones, who suggested, why don't we reach out to the local tribes and see if they have an idea for a name?PRICHEP: And they did - trogloraptor tulishpun. It means cave predator in Sahaptin, the Native language traditionally spoken in the land where the spider was found. But that wasn't the end of things.DONELLA MILLER: I jokingly said - when I said, you know, we're giving the spider a Indian name. That means we're going to have to have the naming ceremony.PRICHEP: Donella Miller is a member of the Yakama Nation and also a biologist with the Columbia River Inter-Tribal Fish Commission, which Binford reached out to to name the spider.MILLER: Honestly, acknowledging the first people, you know, the Indigenous people who live in that area and bringing them into it, it kind of seems like this is what should have been done all along.PRICHEP: This past week, several dozen tribal members and scientists gathered under the hemlocks and fir trees near where the spider was found for a traditional naming ceremony.(SOUNDBITE OF ARCHIVED RECORDING)ANTHONY WASHINES: At this time, we'll open this ground, the sacred ground that we're standing on, and then we'll begin.PRICHEP: Naming ceremonies are usually, unsurprisingly, for people. It's a formal introduction of the name, but it's also a way to sort of welcome that individual and mark their place in the community.(SOUNDBITE OF ARCHIVED RECORDING)WASHINES: You're being a witness to this brother being acknowledged.PRICHEP: Anthony Washines is the Yakima elder who came up with the spider's name.(SOUNDBITE OF ARCHIVED RECORDING)WASHINES: And so, from this day forward, we will call them by the name tulishpun. Repeat after me - tulishpun.UNIDENTIFIED CROWD: Tulishpun.PRICHEP: Gifts and food were shared, and a traditional naming song was sung. A few spiders were gathered to receive their name and then returned back to the nearby caves. Washines knows people will see tulishpun as a small thing. But he says every creature has its place, and this little spider has been in this place even when his people were not.WASHINES: We were literally herded to a reservation up in the high-desert plateau, which was not our land. But he stayed here and remained. He still took care of this land.PRICHEP: Usually, the discovery of a new species is celebrated with a pizza party in the lab, maybe a nod from the dean. It's an academic milestone. But for tulishpun, it's a community event, a gathering of scientists and citizens, of human and animal, to name all of those who make up this land and honor the connections between them.For NPR News, I'm Deena Prichep in Cascade Locks, Oregon.

articles.title: How Pakistan mediated a US-Iran agreement after more than 100 days of war
articles.summary: PM Sharif praises Pakistan's army chief for his efforts in securing a tentative deal, likely to be finalised in Geneva.
articles.titleZh: 巴基斯坦如何在100多天的战争后斡旋美伊协议
articles.summaryZh: 谢里夫总理赞扬巴基斯坦陆军参谋长为达成初步协议所做的努力，该协议很可能在日内瓦敲定。

contentParagraphs.en: Islamabad, Pakistan – Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says there were moments in the final stretch of negotiations between the United States and Iran when the talks appeared close to collapse.Each time, he told the National Assembly on Monday, it was Field Marshal Asim Munir, Pakistan’s powerful military chief, who kept the deliberations alive.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Iran war day 108: Iran, US reach a tentative deal to end conflictlist 2 of 4US, Iran to sign a ‘peace deal’ on Friday: What we knowlist 3 of 4World reacts as Iran and US reach tentative deal to end warlist 4 of 4Iran, US agree tentative deal to ‘end war’: Your questions answeredend of list“Throughout this period, he was awake all day and night,” Sharif told lawmakers, adding that Munir had “sacrificed day and night to extinguish the flames of war”.There were many moments, he said, when “it felt like the negotiations would come to a halt” but the army chief did not give up. “If this journey had not continued,” Sharif said, “the dream of peace would have been shattered.”The acknowledgement, unusually specific for a process conducted almost entirely out of public view, offered the clearest glimpse yet into how Pakistan pulled off what many had considered an improbable task: brokering a deal to end more than three months of a war that has killed thousands of people, mostly in Iran and Lebanon, and disrupted global energy markets.Sharif also praised Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar and his team and Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi for their “tireless efforts” while paying tribute to the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and China for their roles in the mediation.Pakistan’s military and its Ministries of Foreign Affairs and Information and Broadcasting did not respond to Al Jazeera’s requests for details of the US-Iran agreement.‘Extremely difficult circumstances’The agreement, announced early on Monday when Sharif broke the news on X, calls for an immediate and permanent end to military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon.US President Donald Trump confirmed the deal shortly afterwards on his Truth Social platform. “The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete,” he wrote.A signing ceremony hosted by Pakistan is scheduled for Friday in Geneva.Under the 14-point memorandum of understanding, according to Iran’s Mehr News Agency, the US has committed to lifting its naval blockade of Iran within 30 days and withdrawing its forces deployed near Iran.The Strait of Hormuz, which has in effect been shut by Iran since the war began on February 28, is to reopen for normal transit under the agreement.Iran’s frozen assets, estimated at $24bn, are also likely to be released in phases over the ensuing 60 days of further negotiations, during which both sides are expected to address the issue of Iran’s nuclear programme.Discussions on Iran’s missile programme and its support for armed groups have been removed from the immediate agenda, according to the Iranian news agency.The negotiations were conducted under Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, after the elder Khamenei was killed on February 28, the first day of the US-Israel war on Iran.Sharif on Monday specifically named the supreme leader among the leaders who had demonstrated “immense wisdom, prudence and patience under extremely difficult circumstances” during the negotiations.‘Never-give-up approach’Pakistan’s path to the announcement was neither linear nor, by most accounts, straightforward.A Pakistan-brokered ceasefire began on April 8 after Munir made a flurry of calls to US officials in the hours before a Trump deadline to strike Iran expired, and the ceasefire held, but only narrowly. Trump subsequently extended it indefinitely upon the “personal request” of Munir and Sharif, according to Pakistani officials.On April 11 and 12, Pakistan hosted the Islamabad talks, marking the highest-level direct engagement between Washington and Tehran since 1979. But the talks, attended by US Vice President JD Vance, ended without an agreement.For weeks afterwards, face-to-face negotiations did not resume. At one point, Trump said the two sides could speak by phone if needed.Meanwhile, Pakistani officials continued shuttling between Washington, DC, and Tehran, but publicly, there was little indication of progress.Jauhar Saleem, a former Pakistani diplomat, said the arc of Islamabad’s mediation reflected something more fundamental than tactical adjustment.“It’s not a question of what changed between April and June. It’s rather an example of a never-give-up approach in diplomacy where an honest broker respected by both sides can eventually help overcome an overwhelming trust deficit,” Saleem told Al Jazeera.Pakistan’s task, he said, was not only to bridge the gap between the warring parties’ positions but also to help navigate the divide between pragmatists and hardliners within each country, particularly Iran.“Pakistan’s leverage was and remains its credibility as a trusted friend and well-wisher and a fair intermediary,” he said.But Pakistan was not working alone.On March 31, Pakistan and China signed a joint five-point peace plan aimed at ending the war. Beijing’s involvement reflected its concerns over the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, through which much of China’s oil and gas imports pass.In May, Munir travelled to Tehran for a second time. Naqvi, whom Sharif on Monday credited for engaging “with Iranian brothers”, accompanied him.Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi also made multiple visits to Islamabad during the same period, meeting separately with both Munir and Sharif. During one such visit, Araghchi said Tehran intended to engage with Pakistan’s mediators “until a result is achieved”.Final hoursBy Saturday, Dar was speaking with his counterparts in Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt as negotiations entered what Pakistani officials described as their fi
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴基斯坦伊斯兰堡 - 巴基斯坦总理谢赫巴兹·谢里夫表示，在美国和伊朗之间的最后阶段谈判中，有一些时刻，谈判似乎接近崩溃。他在周一向国民议会表示，每次都是巴基斯坦强大的军事参谋长阿西姆·穆尼尔元帅让谈判得以进行。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 伊朗战争第 108 天：伊朗与美国达成一项暂定协议，结束冲突清单 2 4美国、伊朗将于周五签署“和平协议”：我们所知道的 3 of 4 伊朗和美国达成结束战争的临时协议 4 世界的反应 4 伊朗，美国同意“结束战争”的临时协议：你的问题已回答列表末尾“在这段时间里，他昼夜不眠，”谢里夫告诉立法者，并补充说穆尼尔“牺牲了日日夜夜来扑灭战争之火”。有很多时刻，他说，当时“感觉谈判将要停止”，但陆军参谋长并没有放弃。谢里夫说：“如果这一旅程没有继续下去，和平的梦想就会破灭。”这一承认对于一个几乎完全在公众视野中进行的进程来说异常具体，它让我们最清楚地了解巴基斯坦如何完成了许多人认为不可能完成的任务：促成一项协议，结束长达三个多月的战争，这场战争已造成数千人死亡，其中大部分在伊朗和黎巴嫩，并扰乱了全球能源市场。谢里夫还赞扬了副总理兼外交部长伊沙克·达尔和他的团队和内政部长穆赫辛·纳克维的“不懈努力”，同时向卡塔尔、沙特阿拉伯、土耳其和中国领导人在调解中发挥的作用表示敬意。巴基斯坦军方及其外交部、信息和广播部没有回应半岛电视台关于美伊协议细节的要求。谢里夫在 X 上宣布这一消息时，周一早些时候宣布了这项协议，呼吁立即永久停止军事行动。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普不久后在他的“真相社交”平台上确认了这笔交易。他写道：“与伊朗伊斯兰共和国的协议现已完成。”由巴基斯坦主办的签字仪式定于周五在日内瓦举行。据伊朗迈赫尔通讯社报道，根据14点谅解备忘录，美国承诺在30天内解除对伊朗的海上封锁，并撤出部署在伊朗附近的部队。自2月28日战争开始以来实际上已被伊朗关闭的霍尔木兹海峡将根据协议重新开放以正常过境。协议。伊朗冻结的资产估计为240亿美元，也可能在随后60天的进一步谈判中分阶段释放，预计双方将在谈判中解决伊朗核计划问题。据伊朗通讯社报道，有关伊朗导弹计划及其对武装团体支持的讨论已从当前议程中删除。谈判是在接替其父亲阿亚图拉·阿里的伊朗新任最高领袖哈梅内伊领导下进行的哈梅内伊，在老哈梅内伊于2月28日，即美以对伊朗战争的第一天被杀后。谢里夫周一特别点名了在谈判期间表现出“在极其困难的情况下表现出巨大智慧、审慎和耐心”的领导人中的最高领导人。“永不放弃的方针”巴基斯坦宣布这一消息的道路既不是线性的，也不是直截了当的。在穆尼尔匆匆忙忙之后，巴基斯坦斡旋的停火于4月8日开始在特朗普打击伊朗的最后期限到期之前的几个小时内，美国官员接到了很多电话，停火协议也得以维持，但只是勉强维持。据巴基斯坦官员称，特朗普随后应穆尼尔和谢里夫的“个人请求”无限期延长了谈判期限。4月11日至12日，巴基斯坦主办了伊斯兰堡会谈，标志着华盛顿和德黑兰自1979年以来最高级别的直接接触。但美国副总统万斯参加的会谈最终没有达成协议。几周后，面对面的谈判没有恢复。特朗普一度表示，如果需要，双方可以通过电话进行交谈。与此同时，巴基斯坦官员继续在华盛顿和德黑兰之间穿梭，但在公开场合，几乎没有取得任何进展的迹象。前巴基斯坦外交官贾哈尔·萨利姆表示，伊斯兰堡的调解弧线反映了比战术调整更根本的东西。“这不是4月和6月之间发生了什么变化的问题。这是外交上永不放弃的态度的一个例子，诚实的中间人尊重萨利姆告诉半岛电视台。他说，巴基斯坦的任务不仅是弥合交战双方立场之间的分歧，而且还要帮助弥合各国内部实用主义者和强硬派之间的分歧，特别是伊朗。“巴基斯坦的影响力过去是、现在仍然是其作为值得信赖的朋友、祝福者和公平中间人的可信度，”他说。但巴基斯坦并不是单独行动。3月31日，巴基斯坦和中国签署了协议旨在结束战争的五点联合和平计划。北京的介入反映了其对霍尔木兹海峡封锁的担忧，中国大部分石油和天然气进口都要经过霍尔木兹海峡。 今年5月，穆尼尔第二次前往德黑兰。谢里夫周一称赞纳克维“与伊朗兄弟”接触，他陪同他。伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇也在同一时期多次访问伊斯兰堡，分别会见了穆尼尔和谢里夫。在一次这样的访问中，阿拉格奇表示，德黑兰打算与巴基斯坦的调解人进行接触，“直到取得结果”。 周六的最后几个小时，达尔与沙特阿拉伯、土耳其和埃及的同行进行了交谈，谈判进入了巴基斯坦官员所说的“他们的最终目标”。

articles.title: Record daily jump in DRC Ebola outbreak takes death toll to 178
articles.summary: The Ebola outbreak, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus strain, has reached 782 confirmed cases.
articles.titleZh: 刚果民主共和国埃博拉疫情单日激增，死亡人数达到 178 人
articles.summaryZh: 由罕见的本迪布焦病毒株引起的埃博拉疫情已确诊782例。

contentParagraphs.en: The outbreak caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus strain has reached 782 confirmed cases.The number of confirmed cases in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC)’s Ebola outbreak has surged to 782, with 178 deaths, marking one of the largest daily jumps so far as regional conflict, patient escapes, and limited contact tracing undermine containment efforts.The Ministry of Public Health confirmed 72 new cases on Sunday over the previous 24 hours, a record single-day increase, with 29 additional deaths.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3DR Congo arrive in the US for the World Cup after Ebola quarantinelist 2 of 3Alarm as Ebola spreads into new areas of DR Congolist 3 of 3Clashes break out at DRC rally against presidential term-limit changeend of listThe Bundibugyo virus strain has a 22.8 percent death rate so far, with 40 patients recovering, officials said.“We remain committed to supporting affected countries until transmission is stopped. We call on partners and donors to urgently mobilise resources to strengthen the response and save lives,” Jean Kaseya, director general of the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention, said on Sunday.The outbreak stems from the rare Bundibugyo strain, which has no approved vaccine or treatment, unlike the Zaire virus responsible for the DRC’s previous 16 Ebola outbreaks.Contact tracing coverage has plummeted to 56.5 percent, a sharp decline from the 95% target, Health Ministry officials said.Doctors Without Borders, known by its French initials MSF, warned that “no one knows the true scale” of the outbreak due to dangerous gaps in surveillance and testing.Eastern Ituri province remains the outbreak’s epicentre, harbouring nearly 95 percent of all confirmed cases. The virus has since breached into North Kivu and South Kivu provinces and spread across the border to Uganda.Ituri’s humanitarian crisis exacerbates the medical emergency. Nearly one million residents have fled overlapping armed conflicts involving multiple groups, including the M23 rebel movement that controls Goma, the capital of North Kivu province. The area has endured decades of instability, with United Nations reports documenting massacres of more than 100 civilians in gold-rich Ituri villages as various factions vie for control of the region’s mineral wealth.Thousands of artisanal miners routinely shuttle between clandestine mining sites scattered across the mineral-dense region, creating transmission hotspots that evade health monitoring. The outbreak is believed to have originated in the mining-intensive Mongbwalu Health Zone in Ituri province.The World Health Organization announced it is ramping up diagnostic testing and contact surveillance operations. However, MSF reports a critical funding gap of $21.5m hampering response efforts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由罕见的本迪布焦病毒株引起的疫情已确诊病例达到782例。刚果民主共和国埃博拉疫情的确诊病例数已飙升至782例，其中178人死亡，这是迄今为止地区冲突、患者逃逸和有限的接触者追踪破坏遏制工作的最大单日增幅之一。公共卫生部周日在过去24小时内确认了72例新病例，创单日增幅纪录。新增 29 人死亡。 推荐故事清单 3 项清单 3 刚果民主共和国在埃博拉隔离后抵达美国参加世界杯 埃博拉隔离清单 2 为 3 埃博拉蔓延到刚果民主共和国新地区发出警报 3 中的 3 刚果民主共和国集会爆发冲突，反对总统任期限制变更清单结束 官员表示，本迪布焦病毒株迄今为止死亡率为 22.8%，有 40 名患者康复。非洲疾病控制与预防中心总干事让·卡塞亚周日表示：“我们呼吁合作伙伴和捐助者紧急调动资源，加强应对措施并拯救生命。”此次疫情源自罕见的本迪布焦病毒株，与导致刚果民主共和国此前16次埃博拉疫情爆发的扎伊尔病毒不同，该病毒株尚未获得批准的疫苗或治疗方法。接触者追踪覆盖率已大幅下降至56.5%。卫生部官员表示，目标已达到 95%。无国界医生组织（法语缩写为 MSF）警告称，由于监测和检测方面存在危险差距，“没有人知道疫情的真实规模”。东部伊图里省仍然是疫情的震中，占所有确诊病例的近 95%。此后，该病毒侵入北基伍省和南基伍省，并越过边境传播到乌干达。伊图里的人道主义危机加剧了医疗紧急情况。近百万居民逃离了涉及多个团体的重叠武装冲突，其中包括控制北基伍省首府戈马的 M23 反叛运动。该地区经历了数十年的不稳定局势，联合国报告记录了，由于各派别争夺该地区矿产财富的控制权，黄金丰富的伊图里村庄有 100 多名平民遭到屠杀。数千名手工采矿者经常穿梭于散布在矿产密集地区的秘密采矿点之间，形成了逃避健康监测的传播热点。据信，疫情起源于伊图里省采矿密集的蒙布瓦卢卫生区。世界卫生组织宣布正在加强诊断测试和接触者监测行动。然而，无国界医生组织报告称，2150 万美元的严重资金缺口阻碍了应对工作。

articles.title: Social media firms hit back as Starmer announces ban for under-16s in UK
articles.summary:
Meta, YouTube and Snapchat say ban, which would stop children using their platforms, will drive them to ‘less safe services’

UK politics live – latest updates

How will the ban work?

UK parents: how do you feel about the ban?

Britain’s plans to ban social media for under-16s w
articles.titleZh: Starmer 宣布英国禁止 16 岁以下青少年入境，社交媒体公司予以反击
articles.summaryZh:
Meta、YouTube 和 Snapchat 表示，禁令将阻止儿童使用他们的平台，并将迫使他们使用“不太安全的服务”

英国政治直播 – 最新动态

该禁令将如何发挥作用？

英国家长：您对这项禁令有何看法？

英国计划禁止 16 岁以下青少年使用社交媒体

contentParagraphs.en: Britain’s plans to ban social media for under-16s will push teenagers towards more harmful platforms, the world’s biggest technology companies have said as ministers push to enact the new restrictions by next spring.Meta, YouTube and Snapchat have all criticised the ban, which was announced by Keir Starmer on Monday and would stop younger teenagers from using their services.A spokesperson for Meta, which owns Facebook and Instagram, said: “As we’ve seen in Australia, bans risk isolating teens from online communities and information, and driving them to unregulated alternatives that lack built-in protections and parental controls.”YouTube said in a statement: “Blanket bans push kids out of such curated, supervised, beneficial experiences and towards anonymous, less-safe services.”And Snapchat said: “Because the majority of time spent on Snapchat is in private messaging between friends and family, an outright ban that disconnects teens from those relationships doesn’t make them safer – it may simply push them to less safe platforms.”Starmer announced the ban at a Downing Street press conference, despite having previously been sceptical about the idea. Allies say that if he is ousted as prime minister in the coming weeks this will form part of his political legacy.He said: “Social media is making children unhappy, it’s making it easier for bullies to harass and abuse them, and it could even be harming their mental health.”He argued the ban was not anti-tech. “I do not accept, and I will never accept, that you can’t be both pro tech and AI, and at the same time say we must protect our children.”The plan, which goes further than the one enacted earlier this year in Australia, includes a ban on all the main social media platforms, with separate restrictions on online products such as gaming apps, including removing the option to chat to strangers.Under-16s will be prevented from downloading Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X, and from livestreaming themselves. Under-18s will be stopped from using romantic chatbots designed to simulate sexual relationships.“This is not something I do lightly, and I will not present it as cost-free, as if social media has [brought no] benefits to young people, because clearly that is wrong,” Starmer said. “But government is always about choices, and it’s clear to me that a total ban is the right choice.”He said predictions that many teenagers would circumvent the ban, as in Australia, was not the point.“We don’t say: ‘Oh, look, a teenager managed to get a drink somehow, so let’s not bother banning alcohol sales for children.’ We don’t do that, do we?“I just don’t accept that. Our laws are rules, but they’re also an expression of our values. They shape the social contract, and so this will change the conversations that parents have, and the expectations of children over time.“It will make a huge difference. It will make our children safer. It will make our children happier. It will give them more time, more security, full freedom to grow up, more opportunity, and that, at the end of the day, is what this government is about.”The announcement was welcomed by campaigners, many of whom are bereaved parents who say social media played a role in their children’s deaths.Esther Ghey, the mother of the murdered teenager Brianna Ghey, said she was happy about the ban, saying it would “potentially save so many children’s lives”.Starmer made the announcement in front of an audience that included a number of campaigners for a ban, including parents who had lost children, whom he thanked.He said: “I am not prepared to compromise on the safety and happiness of our children, and that is why this ban must happen, and that is why this ban will happen.“Yes, it’s hard – hard to legislate for, hard to regulate, hard to enforce. That’s why we sought a wide range of views on this. That’s why we listened to people, had a conversation, we looked carefully at the evidence, learned from countries like Australia that are taking similar steps.”The government said on Sunday that nine in 10 parents backed a minimum age of 16 for accessing the apps in responses supplied to its “growing up in the online world” consultation.

articles.title: Oil prices drop to cheapest level since early days of Mideast conflict
articles.summary: Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.
articles.titleZh: 油价跌至中东冲突初期以来的最低水平
articles.summaryZh: 由于预期即将达成协议，油价在周四和周五已经大幅下跌。特朗普总统在网上发布消息称，周五签署协议后，霍尔木兹海峡将重新开放。

contentParagraphs.en: A ship remains anchored on May 16, 2026 in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran.
contentParagraphs.zh: A ship remains anchored on May 16, 2026 in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran.

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter, sent every weekday morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter, sent every weekday morning.

contentParagraphs.en: Crude oil prices are down sharply on Monday morning, after President Trump, Iranian leaders and Pakistani negotiators all indicated that a deal to end the war with Iran will be signed on Friday.
contentParagraphs.zh: Crude oil prices are down sharply on Monday morning, after President Trump, Iranian leaders and Pakistani negotiators all indicated that a deal to end the war with Iran will be signed on Friday.

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump posted online about the deal on Sunday evening. Oil futures prices promptly sank around 4%, after markets reopened for trading following their typical weekend break. Prices had already fallen significantly on Thursday and Friday in anticipation of a deal.
contentParagraphs.zh: President Trump posted online about the deal on Sunday evening. Oil futures prices promptly sank around 4%, after markets reopened for trading following their typical weekend break. Prices had already fallen significantly on Thursday and Friday in anticipation of a deal.

contentParagraphs.en: By Monday, prices were down nearly 13% from where it had been in the middle of last week. The cost of one barrel of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, was around $83, and West Texas Intermediate, the U.S. benchmark, around $80. At one point in this conflict, global oil prices had touched $126 a barrel.
contentParagraphs.zh: By Monday, prices were down nearly 13% from where it had been in the middle of last week. The cost of one barrel of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, was around $83, and West Texas Intermediate, the U.S. benchmark, around $80. At one point in this conflict, global oil prices had touched $126 a barrel.

contentParagraphs.en: While oil prices remain elevated compared to pre-war prices, which were in the $60s, they are now cheaper than they have been at any point since the very first days of this conflict.
contentParagraphs.zh: While oil prices remain elevated compared to pre-war prices, which were in the $60s, they are now cheaper than they have been at any point since the very first days of this conflict.

contentParagraphs.en: Cheaper crude oil should push U.S. gasoline prices down, which should in turn help with high levels of inflation. The war in Iran had driven the national average up by as much as $1.50 a gallon; prices have eased in recent weeks, as crude prices dropped on expectations of a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but gasoline remains more than a dollar higher than the pre-war average.
contentParagraphs.zh: Cheaper crude oil should push U.S. gasoline prices down, which should in turn help with high levels of inflation. The war in Iran had driven the national average up by as much as $1.50 a gallon; prices have eased in recent weeks, as crude prices dropped on expectations of a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but gasoline remains more than a dollar higher than the pre-war average.

contentParagraphs.en: Trump's initial post on Sunday evening said he was authorizing "the toll free opening of the Strait of Hormuz," and directed ships to "start your engines." Before the war, approximately 20% of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas passed through that waterway, and the disruption of traffic has caused the greatest oil supply shock in history.
contentParagraphs.zh: Trump's initial post on Sunday evening said he was authorizing "the toll free opening of the Strait of Hormuz," and directed ships to "start your engines." Before the war, approximately 20% of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas passed through that waterway, and the disruption of traffic has caused the greatest oil supply shock in history.

contentParagraphs.en: In a follow-up post, Trump later said that the strait would reopen "upon the signing of the Deal on Friday, for purposes of mine removal."
contentParagraphs.zh: In a follow-up post, Trump later said that the strait would reopen "upon the signing of the Deal on Friday, for purposes of mine removal."

contentParagraphs.en: Markets hope this time, the deal is real
contentParagraphs.zh: Markets hope this time, the deal is real

contentParagraphs.en: Throughout this conflict, oil prices have repeatedly fallen on headlines promising an imminent deal to reopen the strait; however, they've never dropped this low. Significantly, Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who has played a central role negotiating between the U.S. and Iran, has confirmed that a deal has been reached.
contentParagraphs.zh: Throughout this conflict, oil prices have repeatedly fallen on headlines promising an imminent deal to reopen the strait; however, they've never dropped this low. Significantly, Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who has played a central role negotiating between the U.S. and Iran, has confirmed that a deal has been reached.

contentParagraphs.en: "Washington has an incentive to avoid a spike in gasoline prices ahead of the midterms, while Tehran is seeking sanctions relief and restored export revenues, and the global economy has a strong interest in keeping the Strait of Hormuz open," writes Claudio Galimberti, the chief economist for the research firm Rystad Energy, in a note. "On rare occasions, these incentives align in a coherent way, and that is the strongest argument that this is more than another short-lived diplomatic cycle,"
contentParagraphs.zh: "Washington has an incentive to avoid a spike in gasoline prices ahead of the midterms, while Tehran is seeking sanctions relief and restored export revenues, and the global economy has a strong interest in keeping the Strait of Hormuz open," writes Claudio Galimberti, the chief economist for the research firm Rystad Energy, in a note. "On rare occasions, these incentives align in a coherent way, and that is the strongest argument that this is more than another short-lived diplomatic cycle,"

contentParagraphs.en: While risks remain, Galimberti says, a reopening of the strait would begin to reduce global inflationary pressures, which have been mounting.
contentParagraphs.zh: While risks remain, Galimberti says, a reopening of the strait would begin to reduce global inflationary pressures, which have been mounting.

contentParagraphs.en: A rapid reopening of the strait would ease pressure on the world's oil consumers, particularly in Asia and Europe. However, it would not mean an immediate return to pre-war oil supply levels and prices.
contentParagraphs.zh: A rapid reopening of the strait would ease pressure on the world's oil consumers, particularly in Asia and Europe. However, it would not mean an immediate return to pre-war oil supply levels and prices.

contentParagraphs.en: "It could be months before things return to something like the way things were before the war, at least as far as flows out of the Strait of Hormuz go," says Kevin Book, a managing director at Clearview Energy Partners, an independent research firm.
contentParagraphs.zh: "It could be months before things return to something like the way things were before the war, at least as far as flows out of the Strait of Hormuz go," says Kevin Book, a managing director at Clearview Energy Partners, an independent research firm.

contentParagraphs.en: That's because some oil and natural gas production fields and refineries have been taken offline, or damaged in the conflict. "The facilities that have been shut down, some of them can start fairly quickly. Others may take months," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: That's because some oil and natural gas production fields and refineries have been taken offline, or damaged in the conflict. "The facilities that have been shut down, some of them can start fairly quickly. Others may take months," he said.

contentParagraphs.en: Transit takes time, too. Ships also need to move in and out of the strait, and from there around the world.
contentParagraphs.zh: Transit takes time, too. Ships also need to move in and out of the strait, and from there around the world.

contentParagraphs.en: And over the past few months, the world has tapped into its stockpiles of oil in order to make up for missing supplies; refilling those inventories could keep upward pressure on oil prices for months.
contentParagraphs.zh: And over the past few months, the world has tapped into its stockpiles of oil in order to make up for missing supplies; refilling those inventories could keep upward pressure on oil prices for months.

contentParagraphs.en: Before the war began, the world had been oversupplied with oil, which was keeping prices low. Book says it's not clear whether returning to "normal" will mean returning to that status quo.

contentParagraphs.en: "It's not obvious that we'll be in a surplus any time soon," he says.

articles.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
articles.summary: Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.
articles.titleZh: 前美国商人因涉嫌财务不当行为在缅甸被拘留
articles.summaryZh: AGS Myanmar 创始人 Adam Castillo 周四在仰光国际机场被拘留。美国国务院知道有关拘留的报道，但尚未发表进一步评论。

contentParagraphs.en: FILE - U.S. and Myanmar national flags are decorated at University of Yangon's convocation hall in Yangon, Myanmar, Nov. 19, 2012.
contentParagraphs.zh: 文件 - 2012 年 11 月 19 日，缅甸仰光仰光大学集会大厅装饰着美国和缅甸国旗。

contentParagraphs.en: BANGKOK — The former president of the American Chamber of Commerce in Myanmar has been detained upon his return to Yangon after the organization said it was looking into suspicious financial transactions by ex-board members.
contentParagraphs.zh: 曼谷——缅甸美国商会前会长在返回仰光后遭到拘留，此前该组织表示正在调查前董事会成员的可疑金融交易。

contentParagraphs.en: Adam Castillo, a founder and owner of the security risk management firm AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport, according to an associate who spoke on condition of anonymity, citing safety concerns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据一位不愿透露姓名的同事称，安全风险管理公司 AGS Myanmar 的创始人兼所有者 Adam Castillo 周四在仰光国际机场被拘留，理由是安全问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Castillo's company told The Associated Press it was an "ongoing matter" and refused to comment further. Castillo did not respond to an email sent through his personal website.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡斯蒂略的公司告诉美联社，这是一个“持续的问题”，并拒绝进一步置评。卡斯蒂略没有回复通过他的个人网站发送的电子邮件。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. State Department said it was aware of reports that an American had been detained in Myanmar but could not comment further "due to privacy considerations."
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国国务院表示，已获悉一名美国人在缅甸被拘留的报道，但“出于隐私考虑”无法进一步置评。

contentParagraphs.en: Myanmar's military-backed government hasn't released any official statement. The government, the Yangon regional government office and the Yangon Regional Police Department did not answer requests for comment. Authorities in Myanmar, in the midst of civil war, rarely speak to international media.
contentParagraphs.zh: 缅甸军方支持的政府尚未发表任何官方声明。政府、仰光地区政府办公室和仰光地区警察局没有回应置评请求。处于内战之中的缅甸当局很少向国际媒体发表讲话。

contentParagraphs.en: But several outlets close to the military, including NP News, reported that Castillo had been arrested after the American Chamber of Commerce filed a complaint against him. He was president of the organization from 2023-2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但包括《NP News》在内的几家与军方关系密切的媒体报道称，卡斯蒂略在美国商会提出投诉后被捕。他于 2023 年至 2025 年担任该组织主席。

contentParagraphs.en: Asked about the complaint, the chamber's executive director, Myat Phyu The, said she could not give details but said the organization's May 29 annual report "covers the issue at hand." The organization promotes American businesses.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当被问及该投诉时，商会执行董事 Myat Phyu The 表示，她无法透露细节，但表示该组织 5 月 29 日的年度报告“涵盖了当前的问题”。该组织致力于促进美国企业的发展。

contentParagraphs.en: The report says the current board last year uncovered suspect transactions "undertaken by former board representatives" and referred the matter to a law firm for review.
contentParagraphs.zh: 报告称，现任董事会去年发现了“前董事会代表进行的”可疑交易，并将此事提交给律师事务所审查。

contentParagraphs.en: Investigators discovered that "a former board representative" had signed a contract in November 2024 with a Washington-based public relations firm, which paid him $300,000 that was "apparently collected and disbursed outside AMCHAM Myanmar's accounts."
contentParagraphs.zh: 调查人员发现，“一名前董事会代表”于 2024 年 11 月与一家总部位于华盛顿的公关公司签署了一份合同，该公司向他支付了 30 万美元，“显然是在缅甸美国商会账户之外收取和支付的”。

contentParagraphs.en: "The signature exceeded the signing limits of individual board representatives, the board never approved the agreement," the report reads. "AMCHAM Myanmar received no funds, made no payments, and received no services, and the matter was not disclosed to the statutory auditors."
contentParagraphs.zh: 报告称：“签名超出了董事会个别代表的签名限制，董事会从未批准该协议。” “缅甸美国商会没有收到任何资金，没有付款，也没有收到任何服务，此事也没有向法定审计师披露。”

contentParagraphs.en: The report mentions that "two former members of the board" were involved in the case but does not identify either by name or say what legal action was taken by the organization. Myat Phyu The would not elaborate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 报告提到“两名前董事会成员”参与了此案，但没有透露姓名，也没有说明该组织采取了哪些法律行动。 Myat Phyu The 不愿详细说明。

contentParagraphs.en: A June 12 statement on the organization's website said the board "has taken appropriate steps to safeguard the interests of the organization and its members."
contentParagraphs.zh: 该组织网站 6 月 12 日的一份声明称，董事会“已采取适当措施维护该组织及其成员的利益”。

contentParagraphs.en: Myanmar has been wracked by violence since the military ousted democratically elected Aung San Suu Kyi in 2021 and brutally suppressed the nonviolent protests that followed. That triggered armed resistance by pro-democracy guerrillas and ethnic minority militias seeking to oust the military rulers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自2021年军方推翻民选昂山素季并残酷镇压随后的非暴力抗议以来，缅甸一直饱受暴力摧残。这引发了民主游击队和少数民族民兵的武装抵抗，试图推翻军事统治者。

contentParagraphs.en: Since the military's takeover, Myanmar has seen a rise in reported detentions of foreigners, particularly foreign journalists covering the political crisis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自军方接管以来，缅甸拘留外国人的报道有所增加，特别是报道政治危机的外国记者。

contentParagraphs.en: Founded in 2013, AGS Myanmar says on its website that in addition to security, it also provides services including commercial cleaning and pest control.
contentParagraphs.zh: AGS Myanmar成立于2013年，在其网站上表示，除了安保之外，还提供包括商业清洁和害虫防治等服务。

contentParagraphs.en: Castillo's company biography says he is a former U.S. Marine officer who served in Afghanista, and current chair of "Republican Overseas Myanmar," which it says was established in 2024 to promote "America First policies in Myanmar and across the region."
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡斯蒂略的公司简介称，他是一名前美国海军陆战队军官，曾在阿富汗服役，现任“共和党海外缅甸”主席，据称该组织成立于 2024 年，旨在促进“缅甸及整个地区的美国优先政策”。

contentParagraphs.en: It was not immediately clear where Castillo had traveled before returning to Myanmar and being detained. However, posts on his Instagram account show that a day before his arrest he attended a business forum in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, where he also promoted a recently released book.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚卡斯蒂略在返回缅甸并被拘留之前去了哪里。然而，他的 Instagram 帐户上的帖子显示，在被捕前一天，他参加了马来西亚吉隆坡的一个商业论坛，并在那里宣传了一本最近出版的书。

contentParagraphs.en: Castillo's memoir, "Finding Our Voice," recounts his experiences in Myanmar amid political turmoil, violence and economic collapse following the army takeover, according to its synopsis.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡斯蒂略的回忆录《寻找我们的声音》的概要介绍，讲述了他在缅甸军队接管后政治动荡、暴力和经济崩溃的经历。

contentParagraphs.en: It was not clear whether the book played any role in his detention.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚这本书是否在他的拘留中发挥了任何作用。

articles.titleZh: X 拒绝禁止 N 字和 P 字帖子后被指控让种族主义者“有罪不罚”
articles.summaryZh:
该网站没有对针对凯米·巴德诺克、沙巴纳·马哈茂德和齐亚·优素福等英国政客的仇恨帖子采取任何行动
X 拒绝删除数十条被报道为“仇恨、辱骂或骚扰”的社交媒体帖子，其中包括凯米·巴德诺克 (Kemi Badenoch) 在内的英国著名政客

contentParagraphs.en: X has refused to take down dozens of social media posts reported as “hate, abuse or harassment” in which prominent UK politicians, including Kemi Badenoch, have been racially abused.In May, researchers from the social inclusion thinktank British Future reported 30 posts from this year in which the Conservative party leader was called the N-word. In each case the researchers used the platform’s “hate, abuse or harassment” reporting option. X refused to act in the majority of cases, despite repeated requests.The Guardian understands X routinely takes action only when posts are reported to it as illegal under the UK’s Online Safety Act. In those cases, it restricts visibility in the UK, leaving the post unrestricted in other jurisdictions.Avaes Mohammad, a researcher with British Future’s British South Asian Bridgers cohesion project, said: “Many people will intuitively report racist posts as ‘hate, abuse or harassment’ – but it seems X doesn’t consider it as hate. Our research found that it was only when a post is described as illegal, which they can’t dispute, that they will consider taking it down.”X eventually restricted visibility for just two of the 30 messages in which Badenoch was called the N-word – and only after Sunder Katwala, the director of British Future, emailed the platform saying he should not be expected to fill out an “onerous” form reporting each one as illegal.Elon Musk has been using X to amplify far-right narratives since he bought the platform in 2022. Keir Starmer earlier this month accused Musk of trying to “whip up division” with his tweets about the murder of the student Henry Nowak in Southampton.The Online Safety Act, which is being phased in by the communications regulator, Ofcom, places obligations on platforms to remove illegal content, which can include racially or religiously aggravated offending.On 15 May – the same day Ofcom announced X had made a voluntary commitment to remove illegal content within a 48-hour window – British Future reported 33 uses of the P-word targeting UK public figures.Reform UK’s Zia Yusuf was told in one tweet, which used the P-word, to “sod off” to Pakistan. Others who were racially abused included the home secretary, Shabana Mahmood; the former SNP leader, Humza Yousaf; the Reform UK London mayoral candidate, Laila Cunningham; the Advance UK founder, Ben Habib, and the founder of Your Party, Zarah Sultana.Forty-eight hours later, none of the 33 posts reported had been removed.When British Future emailed X to ask why, the platform advised it to report the posts using the “UK illegal content” option from the “report post” menu, adding: “Reports made through other channels are handled under our general policies and do not count toward the specific expedited review targets under the Online Safety Act.”X eventually restricted 20 of the tweets within the UK after being contacted by Ofcom, it is understood, but took no action against the rest. Among messages X left untouched was a picture of a noose tweeted to Sultana, saying “fuck off” and using the P-word.On 26 May, British Future reported another nine posts that used the P-word against British Asian public figures. In six, X failed to act because the posts were reported as “hate, abuse or harassment”. It was only when posts were reported as illegal, in the remaining three cases, that it acted.None of the account holders behind any of the offensive tweets have been suspended, including one who used the N-word 45 times in a week.British Future says the platform’s approach means racists can operate with impunity on X.“The accounts we are reporting are often replete with mentions of Reform and Restore,” Mohammad added. “In May, the N-word racist slur was used about Kemi Badenoch an average of once a day. But on 2 June, after Badenoch responded to Nigel Farage’s speech about Henry Nowak, there were 16 examples in a day – pointing to how much far-right political sentiment is driving the hate on X.”Ofcom said it condemned racism “in all its forms”, and its job was to ensure sites had “appropriate measures in place to comply with their duties” rather than telling them what to take down.The regulator said X had committed “to reviewing and assessing UK suspected illegal terrorist and hate content reported through its dedicated UK illegal content reporting tool” within an average of 24 hours, adding: “We’ll be carrying out quarterly reviews of X’s performance.”X did not respond to requests for comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: As Luigi Mangione's lawyers head to court, support grows for the accused 'vigilante'
articles.summary: Luigi Mangione's legal team is back in court in New York City this week for a key pretrial hearing. He's accused of stalking and killing an insurance CEO. Donors have given $1.5 million to support his defense.
articles.titleZh: 随着路易吉·曼吉奥内的律师出庭，对被告“义务警员”的支持不断增加
articles.summaryZh: 路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 的法律团队本周回到纽约市法庭，参加重要的审前听证会。他被指控跟踪并杀害了一名保险公司首席执行官。捐助者已捐出 150 万美元来支持他的辩护。

contentParagraphs.en: A mural of Luigi Mangione, who is charged with killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare in 2024 in New York, was painted in the Bethnal Green area of London, England. Prosecutors describe Mangione as a ruthless murderer, but the 28-year-old has also drawn support and praise around the world.
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国伦敦贝斯纳尔格林地区绘制了一幅路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 的壁画，他被控于 2024 年在纽约杀害联合医疗保健公司 (UnitedHealthcare) 首席执行官。检察官称曼吉奥内是一名残忍的凶手，但这位 28 岁的人也得到了世界各地的支持和赞扬。

contentParagraphs.en: NEW YORK — As Luigi Mangione's team of attorneys heads back to state court in Manhattan this week for a key pretrial hearing, public support for the 28-year-old continues to grow.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约——随着路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 的律师团队本周返回曼哈顿州法院参加重要的审前听证会，公众对这位 28 岁的人的支持持续增长。

contentParagraphs.en: Some legal experts say Mangione's populist appeal, fueled in part by what many describe as his Instagram-ready good looks, could complicate state and federal trials.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些法律专家表示，曼吉奥内的民粹主义吸引力可能会令州和联邦审判复杂化，而许多人称他的美貌在Instagram上很受欢迎，这在一定程度上推动了他的民粹主义吸引力。

contentParagraphs.en: "The concern you have as a prosecutor is that public support is going to make it into the jury room," said Richard Schoenstein, a legal analyst and defense attorney.
contentParagraphs.zh: 法律分析师兼辩护律师理查德·舍恩斯坦 (Richard Schoenstein) 表示：“作为一名检察官，你担心的是公众的支持会进入陪审团。”

contentParagraphs.en: Mangione is accused of stalking and murdering Brian Thompson, age 50, a health insurance executive and father of two, on a Manhattan street in 2024. Mangione has pleaded not guilty to all charges.
contentParagraphs.zh: Mangione 被指控于 2024 年在曼哈顿街头跟踪并谋杀 50 岁的健康保险高管、两个孩子的父亲 Brian Thompson。Mangione 对所有指控均表示不认罪。

contentParagraphs.en: His crowd-sourced legal defense fund now tops $1.5 million, with more than 42,000 donors. According to a pro-Mangione website created by volunteers, he has also received nearly 7,000 personal letters from dozens of countries around the world.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他的众包法律辩护基金现已超过 150 万美元，捐助者超过 42,000 人。据志愿者创建的支持Mangione的网站称，他还收到了来自全球数十个国家的近7000封私人信件。

contentParagraphs.en: Gary Galperin, a former assistant district attorney in New York County who teaches at Cardozo School of Law, agrees jury selection will be challenging because of Mangione's popularity.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在卡多佐法学院任教的纽约县前助理地区检察官加里·加尔佩林（Gary Galperin）也认为，由于曼吉奥内的受欢迎程度，陪审团的选择将面临挑战。

contentParagraphs.en: "You may come to find that one or more jurors who seemed [unbiased] harbor views that could derail the deliberations," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“你可能会发现，一名或多名看似[公正]的陪审员所持有的观点可能会破坏审议。”

contentParagraphs.en: A CEO's killing, the rise of a folk hero
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位CEO的被杀，一位民间英雄的崛起

contentParagraphs.en: Supporters of Luigi Mangione raise signs outside Manhattan federal court on Jan. 9 in New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1 月 9 日，路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 的支持者在纽约曼哈顿联邦法院外举起标语。

contentParagraphs.en: Another risk, say Schoenstein, Galperin and others, is that some jurors could come to see Mangione's state and federal trials as a referendum on the costly, frustrating and often inaccessible U.S. healthcare system.
contentParagraphs.zh: 舍恩斯坦、加尔佩林等人表示，另一个风险是，一些陪审员可能会将曼吉奥内的州和联邦审判视为对昂贵、令人沮丧且往往难以获得的美国医疗保健系统的公投。

contentParagraphs.en: According to federal prosecutors, a notebook kept by Mangione "contained several handwritten pages that express hostility towards the health insurance industry and wealthy executives in particular."
contentParagraphs.zh: 据联邦检察官称，曼吉奥内保存的笔记本“包含几页手写内容，表达了对健康保险行业，尤其​​是富有的高管的敌意”。

contentParagraphs.en: Mangione's writings allegedly included a plan to "wack" an insurance company CEO.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据称，曼吉奥内的著作中包括一项“攻击”保险公司首席执行官的计划。

contentParagraphs.en: Schoenstein thinks many Mangione supporters are so outraged by U.S. healthcare that they view his alleged violence as a legitimate political statement.
contentParagraphs.zh: 舍恩斯坦认为，许多曼吉奥内的支持者对美国的医疗保健感到非常愤怒，以至于他们认为他所谓的暴力行为是合法的政治声明。

contentParagraphs.en: "There definitely are people out there who assume this defendant committed the crime, but support him in doing so," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “肯定有人认为这名被告犯了罪，但支持他这样做，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: Evan Clarkson, an assistant professor at Utah Valley University who has studied the phenomenon of Mangione's popular support, says he began his research after many of his students told him they felt "conflicted" about Mangione's alleged crimes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 犹他谷大学助理教授埃文·克拉克森（Evan Clarkson）研究了曼吉奥内的民众支持现象，他说，在他的许多学生告诉他，他们对曼吉奥内涉嫌犯罪感到“矛盾”后，他开始了这项研究。

contentParagraphs.en: "There are some students who believe he is absolutely a justified vigilante ... against this system, the American healthcare system, that they think is unjust."
contentParagraphs.zh: “有些学生认为他绝对是一个正义的义务警员……反对他们认为不公正的美国医疗保健制度。”

contentParagraphs.en: Clarkson and other experts think Mangione's political appeal is being further fueled by the fact that he's young and photogenic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 克拉克森和其他专家认为，曼吉奥内年轻且上镜的事实进一步增强了他的政治吸引力。

contentParagraphs.en: Images of Mangione shirtless have gone viral on social media platforms. His fans have written poetry and songs about him and flooded the prison where he's detained with photographs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 曼吉奥内赤裸上身的照片在社交媒体平台上疯传。他的粉丝写了关于他的诗歌和歌曲，并在关押他的监狱里放满了照片。

contentParagraphs.en: Luigi Mangione, accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, attends a court hearing on May 18 to rule on the admissibility of evidence and setting of trial date in New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 被指控杀害联合医疗保健首席执行官布莱恩·汤普森 (Brian Thompson) 的路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 于 5 月 18 日出席纽约法庭听证会，就证据的可采性和审判日期的确定做出裁决。

contentParagraphs.en: Jeenah Moon/pool photo/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Jeenah Moon/泳池照片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Jeenah Moon/pool photo/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Jeenah Moon/泳池照片/法新社盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: "He's hot — and our [research] paper does talk about the role of his physical attractiveness," Clarkson said, noting that views expressed about Mangione's appearance are a "powerful predictor of people's attitudes about him."
contentParagraphs.zh: 克拉克森说：“他很性感，我们的[研究]论文确实谈到了他外表吸引力的作用。”他指出，人们对曼吉奥内外表的看法是“人们对他态度的有力预测”。

contentParagraphs.en: Daniel Byman, an expert on domestic political violence in the U.S. at Georgetown University and a senior fellow at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, agrees Mangione's physical appearance is a significant factor.
contentParagraphs.zh: 乔治敦大学美国家庭政治暴力问题专家、战略与国际研究中心高级研究员丹尼尔·拜曼（Daniel Byman）也认为曼吉奥内的外貌是一个重要因素。

contentParagraphs.en: He compares Mangione's relatively broad cultural support to that of Cuban revolutionary Che Guevara.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他将曼焦内相对广泛的文化支持与古巴革命者切·格瓦拉进行了比较。

contentParagraphs.en: "Che Guevara was a very bloody revolutionary and yet his poster was on dorm room walls," Byman said. "Mangione [like Guevara] is a good-looking guy."
contentParagraphs.zh: “切·格瓦拉是一位非常血腥的革命者，但他的海报却挂在宿舍墙上，”拜曼说。 “马吉奥内（像格瓦拉一样）是一个英俊的小伙子。”

contentParagraphs.en: Mangione's legal team declined to be interviewed for this story, but in a statement posted on a website they created to communicate with supporters, his attorney, Karen Friedman Agnifilo, rejected the idea that her client espoused political violence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 曼吉奥内的法律团队拒绝就此事接受采访，但在他们为与支持者沟通而创建的网站上发布的一份声明中，他的律师凯伦·弗里德曼·阿格尼菲洛否认了她的当事人支持政治暴力的观点。

contentParagraphs.en: State trial scheduled to begin in September
contentParagraphs.zh: 州审判定于九月开始

contentParagraphs.en: In a separate statement, Mangione himself, currently behind bars at the Metropolitan Detention Center in Brooklyn, acknowledged the personal connection many of his supporters feel.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在另一份声明中，目前关押在布鲁克林大都会拘留中心的曼吉奥内本人承认，他的许多支持者感受到了个人联系。

contentParagraphs.en: Luigi Mangione, a suspect in the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, is escorted by police on Dec. 19, 2024, in New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2024 年 12 月 19 日，纽约，联合医疗保健公司 (UnitedHealthcare) 首席执行官布莱恩·汤普森 (Brian Thompson) 被枪杀的嫌疑人路易吉·曼吉奥内 (Luigi Mangione) 在警方护送下。

contentParagraphs.en: "I am overwhelmed by — and grateful for — everyone who has written me to share their stories and express their support," Mangione said in the undated post.
contentParagraphs.zh: 曼吉奥内在这篇未注明日期的帖子中说：“我对每一个写信给我分享他们的故事并表达他们的支持的人感到不知所措和感激。”

contentParagraphs.en: It's unclear how the political and cultural currents surrounding Mangione will play out in court.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前还不清楚围绕曼吉奥内的政治和文化潮流将如何在法庭上发挥作用。

contentParagraphs.en: His team has won significant legal victories. Last September, a state judge tossed out terrorism charges filed against Mangione. Earlier this year, a federal judge ruled that Mangione won't face the death penalty.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他的团队赢得了重大的法律胜利。去年九月，一名州法官驳回了对曼吉奥内提出的恐怖主义指控。今年早些时候，一名联邦法官裁定曼吉奥内不会面临死刑。

contentParagraphs.en: But prosecutors have also won key rulings, including a decision last month by state Judge Gregory Carro to allow crucial pieces of evidence to be presented at trial.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但检察官也赢得了关键裁决，包括上个月州法官格雷戈里·卡罗(Gregory Carro)做出的一项决定，允许在审判中出示关键证据。

contentParagraphs.en: "I found that ruling, at the end of the day, to be a compelling win for the prosecutors," said Schoenstein, the legal analyst. "The gun, the silencer and the notebook [which allegedly belonged to Mangione] are all coming into evidence. It seems like a very strong case for the prosecution."
contentParagraphs.zh: 法律分析师舍恩斯坦表示：“我发现最终的裁决对检察官来说是一场令人信服的胜利。” “枪、消音器和笔记本（据称属于 Mangione）都已成为证据。这对于检方来说似乎是一个非常有力的证据。”

contentParagraphs.en: The state trial is schedu
contentParagraphs.zh: 州审判已安排
