contentParagraphs.en: Good morning. You're reading the Up First newsletter. Subscribe here to get it delivered to your inbox, and listen to the Up First podcast for all the news you need to start your day.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早上好。您正在阅读 Up First 时事通讯。在此订阅，将其发送到您的收件箱，并收听 Up First 播客，了解开始新的一天所需的所有新闻。

contentParagraphs.en: Today's top stories
contentParagraphs.zh: 今日头条新闻

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump, Iran and mediators from Pakistan say a deal has been reached to end the war between the U.S. and Iran. Trump and Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who played a key mediating role in negotiations, both said on social media that an official signing ceremony will take place on Friday. Shehbaz also said that the U.S. and Iran "declared the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon."
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普总统、伊朗和巴基斯坦调解人表示，美国和伊朗之间的战争已经达成协议。特朗普和在谈判中发挥关键调解作用的巴基斯坦总理谢赫巴兹·谢里夫均在社交媒体上表示，正式签字仪式将于周五举行。谢赫巴兹还表示，美国和伊朗“宣布立即永久终止包括黎巴嫩在内的所有战线的军事行动”。

contentParagraphs.en: U.S. President Donald Trump boards Air Force One before departing Joint Base Andrews in Maryland to fly to Evian-les-Bains, France, for the G7 summit on June 15, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普登上空军一号，然后离开马里兰州安德鲁斯联合基地，飞往法国埃维昂莱班，参加 2026 年 6 月 15 日举行的七国集团峰会。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 Iran had made an end to the violence in Lebanon, where Israel is fighting Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants, a condition for a deal. Israel was not part of the negotiations, and NPR's Carrie Kahn tells Up First that a response from Israel's defense minister, Israel Katz, casts doubt on whether the country will cooperate and whether the U.S. has control over its actions.➡️ Oil futures dropped 4% following Trump's announcement. Prices are still elevated compared to before the war, but are now cheaper than they have been at any point since the conflict began. Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will reopen "for purposes of mine removal" after the deal is signed on Friday. While a reopening would ease pressure on the world's oil consumers, it would not mean an immediate return to pre-war oil supply levels and prices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 伊朗已经结束了黎巴嫩的暴力活动，以色列正在黎巴嫩与伊朗支持的真主党武装分子作战，这是达成协议的条件。以色列没有参加谈判，NPR 的 Carrie Kahn 告诉 Up First，以色列国防部长 Israel Katz 的回应让人怀疑该国是否会合作，以及美国是否能控制其行动。➡️ 特朗普宣布这一消息后，石油期货下跌 4%。与战前相比，价格仍然较高，但现在比冲突开始以来的任何时候都便宜。特朗普表示，周五签署协议后，霍尔木兹海峡将“出于排雷目的”重新开放。虽然重新开放将缓解世界石油消费者的压力，但这并不意味着立即恢复到战前的石油供应水平和价格。

contentParagraphs.en: The newly reached agreement will likely dominate the conversation as Trump heads to the French Alps today for the G7 summit. Leaders from the world's wealthiest nations are meeting this week to discuss artificial intelligence, trade, immigration, the war in Ukraine and more. French President Emmanuel Macron, who is hosting the summit, said "the objective will be to see the consequences of this agreement, its support for Lebanon, the longterm reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and of course an agreement on Iran's nuclear and ballistic weapons."
contentParagraphs.zh: 当特朗普今天前往法国阿尔卑斯山参加七国集团峰会时，新达成的协议可能会主导谈话。来自世界上最富有国家的领导人本周举行会议，讨论人工智能、贸易、移民、乌克兰战争等问题。主办这次峰会的法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙表示，“目标是看看这项协议的后果、对黎巴嫩的支持、霍尔木兹海峡的长期重新开放，当然还有关于伊朗核武器和弹道武器的协议。”

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 In April, around 40 countries said they were ready to contribute a force to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz once fighting ends. At the time, Trump disparaged the offer because he wanted those countries' involvement sooner. But NPR's Eleanor Beardsley says Trump is unlikely to lash out at his allies at the summit because, analysts say, he'll be counting on these allies to help give his peace deal more credibility.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 4月，约40个国家表示，一旦战斗结束，他们准备派遣部队帮助重新开放霍尔木兹海峡。当时，特朗普贬低了这一提议，因为他希望这些国家尽快参与。但美国国家公共广播电台的埃莉诺·比尔兹利表示，特朗普不太可能在峰会上猛烈抨击他的盟友，因为分析人士称，他将指望这些盟友帮助他的和平协议更具可信度。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump's trip to France also comes on the tail of a spectacle never before seen at the White House: A UFC fight on the White House Lawn to celebrate his 80th birthday — and the U.S.' 250th. Mixed martial arts fighters went after each other in a 92-foot UFC octagon built on the lawn, hours after Trump announced the peace deal plans. Scenes of fighters warming up inside the Eisenhower Executive Office Building were streamed on Paramount+.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普的法国之行也紧随白宫前所未见的奇观：在白宫草坪上举行 UFC 比赛，以庆祝他和美国的 80 岁生日。第 250 次。在特朗普宣布和平协议计划数小时后，综合格斗选手们在草坪上搭建的 92 英尺 UFC 八角形场地中互相追逐。 Paramount+ 直播了战士们在艾森豪威尔行政办公大楼内热身的场景。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 NPR's Mara Liasson says the event was a celebration, but also a way to contradict messages alleging Trump has been showing signs of decline in health. She notes that the event is happening in the middle of an election all about age and generational change. There's a lot of overlap between the UFC crowd and the young, non-college-educated men who were a critical part of his base, and he's lost support from that base recently. Democrats have publicly criticized the event, saying the president is spending money on personal whims rather than on the problems Americans are actually facing. Liasson says Republicans, meanwhile, are more quietly concerned about Trump's focus on issues that would help them keep their majorities in Congress after the midterms.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 NPR 的玛拉·利亚森 (Mara Liasson) 表示，这次活动是一次庆祝活动，但也是反驳有关特朗普健康状况恶化迹象的信息的一种方式。她指出，这一事件发生在选举中期，一切都与年龄和代际变化有关。 UFC人群和年轻的、未受过大学教育的男性之间有很多重叠，他们是他的基础的重要组成部分，而他最近失去了这个基础的支持。民主党人公开批评这一活动，称总统将钱花在个人的一时兴起上，而不是用于解决美国人实际面临的问题。利亚森表示，与此同时，共和党人更暗地担心特朗普将重点放在有助于他们在中期选举后保持国会多数席位的问题上。

contentParagraphs.en: Twelve people are dead after a plane crashed Sunday in Missouri, killing all on board. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) records show the plane was operated by Skydive Kansas City, which provides skydiving in several locations. The company called the crash a "devastating loss" and said it is cooperating with local and federal investigators. The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) and the FAA are investigating. NTSB investigators are expected to arrive today to begin documenting the scene and examining the aircraft. A preliminary report is expected within 30 days. (via KCUR)
contentParagraphs.zh: 周日，一架飞机在密苏里州坠毁，机上人员全部遇难，造成十二人死亡。美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）的记录显示，这架飞机是由堪萨斯城跳伞公司运营的，该公司在多个地点提供跳伞服务。该公司称此次事故是一次“毁灭性损失”，并表示正在与当地和联邦调查人员合作。美国国家运输安全委员会 (NTSB) 和美国联邦航空局 (FAA) 正在进行调查。 NTSB 调查人员预计将于今天抵达，开始记录现场并检查飞机。初步报告预计将在 30 天内发布。 （通过大韩民国大学）

contentParagraphs.en: Connie Hanzhang Jin/NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: Connie Hanzhang Jin/NPR

contentParagraphs.en: Summer is just around the corner, and so is the deadliest of extreme weather conditions: heat. Extreme temperatures kill more people in the U.S. in an average year than hurricanes, tornadoes and floods combined. Here's the science behind how heat kills — and how you can stay safe this summer.
contentParagraphs.zh: 夏天即将来临，最致命的极端天气条件也即将到来：高温。在美国，平均每年死于极端气温的人数比飓风、龙卷风和洪水造成的死亡人数总和还多。以下是高温如何致命的科学原理，以及如何在今年夏天保持安全。

contentParagraphs.en: ☀️ Your body tries to cool down by sweating and redirecting more blood to the skin. But that means less blood goes to your gut. If this persists for a prolonged period, it could lead to multiple organ failure.☀️ Arteries widen, and blood pressure drops as your body pumps blood to the skin, making your heart work harder. The stress can trigger heart attacks or strokes.☀️ Sweating makes you dehydrated. If you don't replenish your fluids, it can be hard on your kidneys and lead to kidney failure.☀️ Drink plenty of water before you head into the heat, and every 15-20 minutes if you're physically active.☀️ If you start to feel overheated, wet your clothes and skin, seek shade or head to a cool spot indoors.
contentParagraphs.zh: ☀️ 您的身体试图通过出汗并将更多血液引导至皮肤来降温。但这意味着流向肠道的血液更少。如果这种情况持续很长一段时间，可能会导致多器官衰竭。☀️动脉扩张，血压下降，因为你的身体将血液泵送到皮肤，使你的心脏更加努力地工作。压力会引发心脏病或中风。☀️出汗会让你脱水。如果不补充水分，可能会对肾脏造成负担，并导致肾衰竭。☀️ 在进入高温环境之前喝大量的水，如果进行体力活动，则每 15-20 分钟喝一次。☀️ 如果您开始感觉过热，弄湿衣服和皮肤，请寻找阴凉处或前往室内凉爽的地方。
